name: City of Ávila by the Río Adaja — circular
name_original: Ciudad de Ávila por el Río Adaja — circular
region_intro: Jogging around Ávila offers a unique high-altitude experience, with the city situated over 1100 meters above sea level. The region features a diverse landscape, from historical urban settings with ancient city walls to the natural beauty of the Sierra de Gredos mountain range and the Alberche River Valley. Runners can find varied terrain, including paved paths for easy runs and more challenging, uneven trails with significant elevation changes. This combination provides a compelling destination for outdoor activities, catering to different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Ávila The most popular jogging route is City of Ávila by the Río Adaja — circular, an easy 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 29 minutes to complete. This route offers views along the Adaja River, complementing the city's historic backdrop. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Escuela Nacional de Policía, an easy 2.4 miles (3.9 km) path. This route provides a straightforward run through urban and suburban areas. Local runners also love the Walls of Ávila loop from Ávila, a difficult 11.0 miles (17.8 km) trail leading around the historic city walls, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Jogging around Ávila is defined by historic city walls, river valleys, and the foothills of the Sierra de Gredos. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy urban loops to more demanding mountain trails. The routes in Ávila are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 6 reviews. More than 990 runners have used komoot to explore Ávila's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available around Ávila?
A: There are over 80 running routes available around Ávila, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include easy urban loops, moderate river valley paths, and challenging mountain trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes near Ávila?
A: Ávila offers a diverse range of terrain for runners. You'll find mostly paved surfaces suitable for easy runs, accessible paths, and more challenging, uneven terrain with steep sections, rocks, and roots, especially in areas closer to the Sierra de Gredos. The region's high altitude, over 1100 meters, also adds a unique dimension to the running experience.

Q: Are there running options for different fitness levels in Ávila?
A: Yes, Ávila provides running options for all fitness levels. There are 16 easy routes, 57 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes. For an easy run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159323650/city-of-avila-by-the-rio-adaja-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City of Ávila by the Río Adaja — circular&lt;/a&gt;. For a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31473469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walls of Ávila loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; offers a difficult 17.8 km trail.

Q: Can I run near the historic Walls of Ávila?
A: Absolutely! The remarkably preserved medieval Walls of Ávila, a UNESCO World Heritage site, provide a unique and inspiring backdrop for runs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31473469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walls of Ávila loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 17.8 km trail that takes you around this historic landmark, offering incredible views of the city and its surroundings.

Q: Are there routes with scenic viewpoints of Ávila?
A: Yes, several routes offer stunning panoramic views. The monument of Los Cuatro Postes, just outside the city, provides one of the best vantage points of Ávila and its magnificent walls, especially beautiful at sunset. Joggers can incorporate a visit to this viewpoint for a rewarding scenic experience.

Q: What natural features can I explore while running near Ávila?
A: Beyond the city, you can explore the natural beauty of the Sierra de Gredos mountain range, the Alberche River Valley, and the Embalse del Rosarito. These areas offer diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain trails to serene river loops. You might also encounter highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5621589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Canchal&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6162407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running paths in Ávila?
A: For families looking for an easier run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159323650/city-of-avila-by-the-rio-adaja-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City of Ávila by the Río Adaja — circular&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. It's an easy 4.4 km trail along the Adaja River, offering pleasant views without significant elevation changes, making it suitable for a leisurely family outing.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running routes in Ávila?
A: Many of the natural trails and paths around Ávila are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept under control. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Viejo de Ávila loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7 km route, offers open spaces that can be enjoyable for both you and your canine companion.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Ávila?
A: The running routes in Ávila are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 6 reviews. Runners often praise the unique combination of historical backdrops, like the city walls, and the diverse natural landscapes, from river valleys to mountain foothills. The variety of routes catering to different abilities is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there circular running routes available near Ávila?
A: Yes, many routes around Ávila are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159323650/city-of-avila-by-the-rio-adaja-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City of Ávila by the Río Adaja — circular&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.4 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31451323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adaja River Trail loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate 9.8 km circular option.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Ávila?
A: Ávila's high-altitude environment means it can be cooler than surrounding areas. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant running conditions with mild temperatures. Summer mornings and evenings are also good, but midday can be hot. Winters can be cold, but clear days can still be enjoyable for a run.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical sites or points of interest?
A: Many routes in and around Ávila offer glimpses of its rich history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31473469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walls of Ávila loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; directly engages with the city's most iconic historical monument. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Roman bridge over the Adaja River&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1034227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of San Vicente of Ávila&lt;/a&gt; on various routes.
tips: The Walls of Ávila are an impressive medieval fortification that surrounds the old town of Ávila, in Castile and León. These walls, built in the 11th century, are one of the best-preserved walled enclosures in Spain and were declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1985.

Aside from archaeological evidence pointing to a primitive fence from the Late Antiquity (5th century) period, which would have enclosed a small space, the wall is essentially medieval. With a perimeter of 2,516 m (encompassing an area of 33 hectares), 87 towers or towers, 9 gates and 2 gatehouses, and 2,500 merlons, Ávila's wall is the best-preserved walled enclosure in the world.

This bridge was built to facilitate crossing the Adaja River and connect the Roman city of Ávila with the opposite bank. Although only the hidden abutments on the edges of the original Roman bridge remain, the bridge visible today dates back to the 12th century.

waypoints.name: Old Roman bridge over the Adaja River
